topic: Write and Edit tools across AI agent ecosystems — autonomous coders, frameworks, and tool-use methodologies
scopeNote: Complements agent-write-edit-tools.md (dev harnesses). This guide covers autonomous agents (OpenHands, SWE-agent, Devin, Agentless), frameworks (AutoGPT, BabyAGI, CrewAI, LangChain, LangGraph/deepagents, Autogen), and methodology lineage (Hermes, OpenAI Agents SDK ApplyPatchTool, MCP filesystem servers).
confidenceNote: Confidence rating combines source authority, code-level verification (did we read the actual implementation?), and recency. High = source code or paper examined directly. Medium = official docs examined. Low = indirect reference or source returned partial content.

sources.title: OpenHands str_replace_editor tool definition
keyInsights: Five commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, undo_edit — direct port of Anthropic text_editor schema
old_str must EXACTLY match one or more consecutive lines including whitespace
Uniqueness required; recommends 3-5 lines of context for disambiguation
create cannot overwrite existing files
Persistent state across calls (enables undo_edit)
Absolute paths required; binary-aware preview (Excel/PowerPoint/PDF/Word)

sources.title: OpenHands function_calling.py — tool registration
keyInsights: Two file-edit tools: deprecated LLMBasedFileEditTool (V0) and current str_replace_editor_tool
V0 is scheduled for removal April 2026 (migration to V1 agentic architecture)
LLMBasedFileEditTool takes path/content/start/end/impl_source — entire region regeneration
str_replace_editor_tool created via create_str_replace_editor_tool()

sources.title: OpenHands: An Open Platform for AI Software Developers as Generalist Agents (ICLR 2025)
keyInsights: ICLR 2025 paper; platform for generalist AI software-developer agents
CodeActAgent core: code, CLI, browser interaction
Evaluation covers 15 tasks including SWE-bench
Full paper details behind PDF — implementation specifics require repo

sources.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
keyInsights: Coins the term Agent-Computer Interface (ACI)
12.5% pass@1 on SWE-bench; 87.7% on HumanEvalFix
Thesis: LM agents are a new end-user category requiring purpose-built interfaces
ACI covers file create/edit, repo navigation, test execution

sources.title: SWE-agent Documentation — Agent-Computer Interface background
keyInsights: Windowed file viewer optimal at 100 lines per turn
Lint-gate on edit: syntax-invalid edits are rejected
Full-directory string search lists files with matches (succinct)
Returns explicit 'ran successfully and did not produce any output' on silent commands

sources.title: SWE-agent windowed_edit_linting tool bundle config
keyInsights: Edit command signature: `edit <start_line>:<end_line>\n<text>\nend_of_edit`
Parameters: start_line (int), end_line (int, inclusive), replacement_text (string)
Indentation is the caller's responsibility
Multiple tool bundles: edit_linting, edit_anthropic, windowed, windowed_edit_*

sources.title: CodeAct: Executable Code Actions Elicit Better LLM Agents
keyInsights: Advocates Python code actions over JSON/text tool-call format
CodeActAgent finetuned from Llama2/Mistral; basis for OpenHands' agent design
Action space consolidation argument — arbitrary Python beats predefined JSON

sources.title: Agentless: Demystifying LLM-based Software Engineering Agents
keyInsights: Three phases: localization, repair, validation — no agentic loop
32% on SWE-bench Lite at $0.70/task
Counter-argument to agentic editing for this task class

sources.title: Agentless repair phase source
keyInsights: Three edit formats: edit_file(filename, start, end, content), SEARCH/REPLACE, str_replace (Anthropic-only)
Sampling: greedy T=0 + additional T=0.8 up to max_samples
Prompt caching across samples
Fails are logged and loop continues; no re-sample trigger, just draw from next candidate

sources.title: Cognition AI — Devin SWE-bench technical report
keyInsights: Devin operates with shell, editor, browser in sandboxed VM
Evaluation method: extract final filesystem diffs as patch
Internal editor + repair loop details undisclosed

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK (Python) — Tools overview
keyInsights: Hosted tools: WebSearchTool, FileSearchTool, CodeInterpreterTool, HostedMCPTool, ImageGenerationTool, ToolSearchTool
Local tools: ComputerTool, ShellTool, ApplyPatchTool
ApplyPatchTool works via ApplyPatchEditor protocol implementation
@function_tool decorator is the primary custom-tool mechanism

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK tool.py — ApplyPatchTool definition
keyInsights: ApplyPatchTool is a dataclass with needs_approval (bool | callable) and on_approval (callback)
ApplyPatchEditor and ApplyPatchOperation imported from .editor
ApplyPatchApprovalFunction: (ctx, op, patch_str) -> {approve: bool, reason?: str}
Approval gating is first-class

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK API reference — tool module
keyInsights: ApplyPatchTool has needs_approval and on_approval fields
ApplyPatchOnApprovalFunction callback returns ApplyPatchOnApprovalFunctionResult

sources.title: GPT-5 New Parameters and Tools Cookbook
keyInsights: Freeform function calling: raw text payloads without JSON wrapping
Relevant because V4A patches can be modeled as freeform custom tools
Also covers verbosity parameter, CFG, minimal reasoning mode

sources.title: MCP Filesystem server README
keyInsights: write_file(path, content) — whole-file overwrite, flagged as dangerous
edit_file(path, edits[{oldText, newText}], dryRun?) — multi-edit with git-style diff output
Allowed-directories sandboxing via CLI + MCP Roots protocol
read_text_file supports head/tail; search_files has excludePatterns

sources.title: MCP Filesystem server lib.ts — applyFileEdits implementation
keyInsights: Sequential edit application in memory; fail-fast before disk write
Exact-match first, then line-by-line whitespace-tolerant fallback
Line-ending normalization on old/new text and file content
Diff via createTwoFilesPatch with dynamic backtick escaping
Atomic write via temp file + rename (defeats symlink race)
Throws 'Could not find exact match for edit:' on miss

sources.title: NousResearch Hermes-Function-Calling repository
keyInsights: Four XML tags: <tools>, <tool_call>, <tool_response>, <scratch_pad>
Tool call payload: JSON object {name, arguments}
Focus: financial data retrieval demo (yfinance); no file-edit tools
validate_and_extract_tool_calls + validate_function_call_schema pipeline

sources.title: NousResearch hermes-agent
keyInsights: Provider-agnostic agent harness (OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter/NVIDIA)
40+ tools; six terminal backends (local/Docker/SSH/Daytona/Singularity/Modal)
Subagent delegation, cron scheduling, closed learning loop
Not model-specific despite the name

sources.title: CrewAI FileWriterTool source
keyInsights: Parameters: filename, directory (default './'), overwrite (default False), content
Whole-file writer only — no diff/edit support
Refuses to overwrite by default; uses 'w' or 'x' mode based on overwrite flag
No read-before-write, no atomicity (no temp file), no backup
Auto-creates directory via os.makedirs

sources.title: LangChain FileManagementToolkit overview
keyInsights: Seven tools: CopyFileTool, DeleteFileTool, FileSearchTool, MoveFileTool, ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, ListDirectoryTool
Whole-file only; no edit/diff tool
root_dir sandboxing strongly recommended
Docs explicit: without root_dir, easy for LLM to pollute working directory

sources.title: LangChain deepagents
keyInsights: Claude-Code-inspired open-source agent framework built on LangGraph
File tools: read_file, write_file, edit_file, ls, glob, grep + write_todos planner
execute tool for shell access
Sub-agent delegation, automatic context summarization
MIT licensed

sources.title: Microsoft Autogen Magentic-One + FileSurfer docs
keyInsights: Magentic-One Coder agent uses code executors (Docker/Local) for file writes — no dedicated FileEditor
FileSurfer: read-only agent with open_path, page_up, page_down, find_on_page_ctrl_f, find_next
Default 5120-character viewport in FileSurfer
File edits happen via shell commands generated by the Coder agent

gaps: Devin internals are proprietary; comparison is indirect

Claude Agent SDK autonomous patterns not deeply explored — the scope prompt listed it but the Agents SDK (OpenAI) and Claude Code / Agent SDK (Anthropic) have different autonomous surfaces; we focused on OpenAI's because it has ApplyPatchTool as a library primitive
'Claw' is not a publicly documented Nous Research framework; the user may have had a specific internal artifact in mind. Guide explicitly flags this.
ChatDev and other multi-agent dev simulators have minimal public detail on edit tool semantics

Detailed benchmark-by-benchmark SWE-bench numbers for OpenHands, SWE-agent latest runs not exhaustively compiled — the existing Aider leaderboard and SWE-bench.com entries cover this, and the focus here was on tool semantics not leaderboard rankings
